LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANNE ROLLANDI & LARAMAR URBAN APARTMENTS PARTNERS' PETITION FOR AN ORDER TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THERE IS AN AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. IT IS BROAD ENOUGH TO COVER ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. THE ACTION IS STAYED AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS AND THE ENTIRE CASE IS ORDERD TO ARBITRATION. EMPLOYER TO PAY ADMINISTRATIVE COST OF ARBITRATION, ARBITRATION FEE AND TRAVEL EXPENSES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ANNE ROLLANDI & LARAMAR URBAN APARTMENTS PARTNERS' PETITION FOR AN ORDER TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MARCH 15, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ARCHWAY PROPERTY SERVICES, LLC AND ANDREW HAWKINS-COHEN & DOUGLAS BURROWS' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY COURT PROCEEDINGS AGAINST DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THERE IS AN AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. IT DOES NOT COVER ALL OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT. THE ARCHWAY AGREEMENT IS PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON THE LARAMAR AND ROLLANDI MOTION, THE ACTION IS STAYED AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS AND THE ENTIRE CASE IS ORDERD TO ARBITRATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Petition For An Order To Compel Arb
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicole A. Bolson, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, Tel. 415.544.1055, for defendants and moving party Laramar & Rollandi;
Anna C. Gehringer, Esq. of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Petra M. Reineckie, Esq. of PMR, Tel. 415.591.1102, for defendants Archway, etal.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: DEFENDANTs ANNE ROLLANDI & LARAMAR URBAN APARTMENTS PARTNERS' Petition For An Order To Compel Arbitration is granted. There is an agreement to arbitrate. It is broad enough to cover all causes of action alleged in the complaint. The action is stayed as to all defendants and the entire case is orderd to arbitration. Employer to pay administrative cost of arbitration, arbitration fee and travel expenses. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Court Proceedings Against Defts
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Petra M. Reineckie, Esq. of PMR, Tel. 415.591.1102, for defendant and moving party Archway, etal;
Anna C. Gehringer, Esq. of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Nicole A. Bolson, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, Tel. 415.544.1055, for defendants Laramar & Rollandi.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: DEFENDANTs ARCHWAY PROPERTY SERVICES, LLC and ANDREW HAWKINS-COHEN & DOUGLAS BURROWS' Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Court Proceedings Against Defendants is denied. There is an agreement to arbitrate. It does not cover all of the causes of action in the complaint. The Archway agreement is procedurally and substantively unconscionable. In light of the court's ruling on the Laramar and Rollandi motion, the action is stayed as to all defendants and the entire case is orderd to arbitration. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Court Proceedings Against Defts

MINI MINUTES:

Petra M. Reinecke, Esq. of PMR, Tel. 415.591.1102, for defendants and moving party;
Anna C. Gehriger, Esq. Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, Tel. 415.278.9400, for plaintiff;
Nicole A. Bolson, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, Tel. 415.544.1055, for defendants Rollandi & Laramar.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, DEFENDANTs ARCHWAY PROPERTY SERVICES LLC, ANDREW HAWKINS-COHEN, & DOUGLAS BURROWS' Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Court Proceedings Against Defendants is continued to March 19, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 for further hearing. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Petra M. Reinecke, Esq. of PMR, Tel. 415.591.1102, for defendants Archway, Hawkins-Cohen & Burrows.
